---
title: "Tamoxifen + dextromethorphan / CYP2D6 / CYP3A phenotyping (TerHeine 2014)"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Tamoxifen + dextromethorphan / CYP2D6 / CYP3A phenotyping (TerHeine 2014)}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(nlmixr2lib)
library(PKNCA)
library(rxode2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

# Models and source

ter Heine 2014 (Br J Clin Pharmacol 78(3):572-586,
doi:[10.1111/bcp.12388](https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.12388)) developed two
linked population pharmacokinetic models in 39 breast-cancer patients on
chronic tamoxifen:

1. A semi-physiological eight-compartment model for the CYP2D6 / CYP3A
   probe drug dextromethorphan and its three phase I metabolites
   (dextrorphan, 3-methoxymorphinan, 3-hydroxymorphinan; Table 2 +
   Appendix 1 of the source). Individual post-hoc CYP2D6 and CYP3A
   clearances are extracted from this model and used as covariates in
   the tamoxifen model.

2. A joint tamoxifen / endoxifen popPK model with the dextromethorphan-
   probe-derived CYP2D6 (CL_CYP2D6,1) and CYP3A (CL_CYP3A,1) activity
   scores as power-law covariates on the endoxifen-formation clearance
   (Table 3 + Appendix 2). Endoxifen disposition is fixed from Ahmad
   et al. (2010) Clin Pharmacol Ther 88(6):814-817 for identifiability.

Both `.R` files for this paper point to this vignette via the same
`vignette <- "TerHeine_2014_tamoxifen"` slot:

```{r model-handles}
mod_dex <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("TerHeine_2014_dextromethorphan"))
mod_tam <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("TerHeine_2014_tamoxifen"))

cat("Dextromethorphan model:\n", mod_dex$description, "\n\n", sep = "")
cat("Tamoxifen model:\n",        mod_tam$description, "\n",   sep = "")
```

The tamoxifen model also has a DDMORE Foundation Model Repository entry
(`DDMODEL00000212`); the parameter values in the tamoxifen file come from
the paper's Table 3 (transcribed via the DDMORE bundle's
`Output_real_tamoxifen.pdf` screenshot). The dextromethorphan model is
extracted directly from the source publication's Table 2 + Appendix 1.

# Population

39 evaluable breast-cancer patients on chronic oral tamoxifen at the
Erasmus MC Cancer Institute (Rotterdam, The Netherlands; Dutch Trial
Registry NTR1751). Table 1 of the source publication:

| Variable     | Median | Range       |
|--------------|--------|-------------|
| Age (years)  | 53     | 22 - 71     |
| Height (m)   | 1.69   | 1.56 - 1.79 |
| Weight (kg)  | 72.7   | 48.5 - 114  |

Tamoxifen dose: 20 mg orally QD in 70% of subjects, 40 mg QD in 30%
(11 of 39 evaluable subjects on the higher dose). Dextromethorphan was
given as a single 30 mg oral dose 2 hours after the daily tamoxifen
dose. Patients on moderate or strong CYP3A inhibitors / inducers or
ABCB1 / ABCG2 modulators were excluded. 40 patients enrolled, 1 dropped
due to venous-cannula obstruction.

The same information is available programmatically via
`readModelDb("TerHeine_2014_tamoxifen")$population` and
`readModelDb("TerHeine_2014_dextromethorphan")$population`.

# Source trace

The per-parameter origin is recorded as an in-file comment next to each
`ini()` entry in `inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/TerHeine_2014_dextromethorphan.R`
and `inst/modeldb/ddmore/TerHeine_2014_tamoxifen.R`. The tables below
collect them in one place for review.

## Dextromethorphan model (paper Table 2, Appendix 1)

| Parameter (paper symbol) | Value | Units | Source location |
|--------------------------|-------|-------|-----------------|
| `lka` (k12)              | 0.213 | 1/h   | Table 2 (RSE 7.6%) |
| `ltlag` (tlag)           | 0.369 | h     | Table 2 (RSE 3.7%) |
| `lvc` (V_dex,central)    | 188   | L     | Table 2 (RSE 32.4%) |
| `lvp` (V_dex,peripheral) | 1660  | L     | Table 2 (RSE 16.8%) |
| `lvc_metab`              | 419   | L     | Methods (FIXED; Abduljalil 2009) |
| `lq` (Q1)                | 415   | L/h   | Table 2 (RSE 22.1%) |
| `lq2` (Q2)               | 200   | L/h   | Table 2 (RSE 37.3%) |
| `lcl_2d6_1` (CL_CYP2D6,1)| 1560  | L/h   | Table 2 (RSE 27.8%) |
| `lcl_2d6_2` (CL_CYP2D6,2)| 362   | L/h   | Table 2 (RSE 46.1%) |
| `lcl_3a4_1` (CL_CYP3A,1) | 44.7  | L/h   | Table 2 (RSE 26%) |
| `lcl_3a4_2` (CL_CYP3A,2) | 1840  | L/h   | Table 2 (RSE 9.1%) |
| `lcl_hm` (CL_HM)         | 5730  | L/h   | Table 2 (RSE 11.4%) |
| `lk57`                   | 1.11  | 1/h   | Table 2 (RSE 19.5%) |
| `lk75`                   | 0.0815| 1/h   | Table 2 (RSE 28.7%) |
| `lk68`                   | 13.4  | 1/h   | Table 2 (RSE 12.4%) |
| `lk86`                   | 0.0637| 1/h   | Table 2 (RSE 18.5%) |
| `etalka`                 | 31%   | CV    | Table 2 (RSE 39.7%) |
| `etalcl_2d6` (shared)    | 166%  | CV    | Table 2 (RSE 29.5%) |
| `etalcl_3a4_1`           | 89.8% | CV    | Table 2 (RSE 49.3%) |
| `etalcl_3a4_2`           | 53.0% | CV    | Table 2 (RSE 27.8%) |
| `etalcl_hm`              | 75.7% | CV    | Table 2 (RSE 24.6%) |
| `propSd`     (dex)       | 0.429 | -     | Table 2 (RSE 15%) |
| `propSd_dxor`            | 0.400 | -     | Table 2 (RSE 10.6%) |
| `propSd_3mm`             | 0.297 | -     | Table 2 (RSE 23.2%) |
| `propSd_3hm`             | 0.307 | -     | Table 2 (RSE 12%) |
| Differential equations   | n/a   | n/a   | Appendix 1 of the source paper |

## Tamoxifen model (paper Table 3, Appendix 2)

| Parameter (paper symbol)             | Value  | Units | Source location |
|--------------------------------------|--------|-------|-----------------|
| `lka` (k12)                          | 1.90   | 1/h   | Table 3 (RSE 20.2%) |
| `ltlag` (tlag)                       | 0.455  | h     | Table 3 (RSE 10.4%) |
| `lcl` (CL_TAM = CL20/F)              | 9.34   | L/h   | Table 3 (RSE 6.20%) |
| `lvc` (Vd_tam = V2/F)                | 753    | L     | Table 3 (RSE 9.00%) |
| `lq` (Q1/F)                          | 61.8   | L/h   | Table 3 (RSE 65.4%) |
| `lcl_endx` (CL_MET = CL23/F)         | 0.324  | L/h   | Table 3 (RSE 17.0%) |
| `e_cyp2d6_cl_endx` (theta_CYP2D6,1)      | 0.262  | -     | Table 3 (RSE 14.0%) |
| `e_cyp3a4_cl_endx` (theta_CYP3A,1)       | 0.157  | -     | Table 3 (RSE 72.0%) |
| `etalcl` (CL_TAM)                    | 37.8% CV -> 0.1336 (log-var) | Table 3 (RSE 19.2%) |
| `etalvc` (Vd_tam)                    | 26.6% CV -> 0.0683 (log-var) | Table 3 (RSE 53.9%; paper reports 26.7%, file uses 26.6%) |
| rho(CL_TAM, Vd_tam) -> block covariance | 0.0586 | -     | Table 3 (RSE 31.2%; rho ~ 61.3% per file vs 61.2% in Table 3) |
| `etalcl_endx`                        | 25.5% CV -> 0.0630 (log-var) | Table 3 (RSE 19.3%; paper reports 25.4%, file uses 25.5%) |
| `propSd`     (tam)                   | 0.138  | -     | Table 3 (RSE 11.3%; paper reports 13.7%, file uses 13.8%) |
| `propSd_endx`                        | 0.189  | -     | Table 3 (RSE 10.1%) |
| Endoxifen Vc (FIXED)                 | 400    | L     | Methods; Ahmad et al. (2010) CPT 88(6):814-817 |
| Endoxifen CL (FIXED)                 | 5.1    | L/h   | Methods; Ahmad et al. (2010) CPT 88(6):814-817 |
| Tamoxifen MW                         | 371.5  | g/mol | DDMORE bundle GROUP_VARIABLES |
| Endoxifen MW                         | 373.5  | g/mol | DDMORE bundle GROUP_VARIABLES |
| CYP2D6 reference                     | 1560   | ng/L  | DDMORE bundle (population median; matches paper Table 2 CL_2D6,1) |
| CYP3A4 reference                     | 44.7   | ng/L  | DDMORE bundle (population median; matches paper Table 2 CL_3A4,1) |
| Differential equations               | n/a    | n/a   | Appendix 2 of the source paper |

The half-dozen 0.1%-level off-by-rounding discrepancies between the
tamoxifen `.R` file and the source paper's Table 3 (V_d 26.6% vs 26.7%,
rho 0.613 vs 0.612, IIV CL_MET 25.5% vs 25.4%, sigma_TAM 0.138 vs 0.137 from
paper-reported 13.7%) are inherited from the original DDMORE-bundle
transcription. They are within the paper's three-significant-figure
reporting precision and are flagged in this table for transparency rather
than corrected, to preserve provenance against the existing DDMORE
extraction.

# Dextromethorphan model -- typical-value simulation

A single 30 mg oral dose of dextromethorphan over the paper's 22-hour
post-dose sampling window. The model is a typical-value semi-
physiological PK structure with no estimated covariates (its purpose is
to *produce* individual CYP2D6 and CYP3A activity scores from the
sampled dextromethorphan / metabolite concentration-time curves; those
scores feed downstream into the tamoxifen model).

```{r dex-simulate}
set.seed(42)

# One subject, single 30 mg oral dose at t = 0. Observation grid covers
# the paper's pre-dose to 22 h sampling window.
# Multi-output: dose targets depot (cmt = 1); we anchor observation rows
# at the first model output (cmt = 9 = "Cc") and read the other three
# derived outputs (Cc_dxor / Cc_3mm / Cc_3hm) from the same simulation row
# (rxode2 evaluates all algebraic outputs at every step).
obs_t <- c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 10, 14, 18, 22)

ev_dex <- rbind(
  data.frame(id = 1, time = 0,     evid = 1, amt = 30, cmt = 1L),
  data.frame(id = 1, time = obs_t, evid = 0, amt = 0,  cmt = 9L)
)

mod_dex_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod_dex)

sim_dex <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_dex_typ, events = ev_dex)
sim_dex <- as.data.frame(sim_dex)
sim_dex$id <- 1L  # rxSolve drops id when there's a single subject
sim_dex <- sim_dex |>
  dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, Cc_dxor, Cc_3mm, Cc_3hm)
```

```{r dex-profile, fig.width=7, fig.height=4}
sim_dex_long <- sim_dex |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Cc, Cc_dxor, Cc_3mm, Cc_3hm),
    names_to = "species", values_to = "conc_nM"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(species = factor(
    species,
    levels = c("Cc", "Cc_dxor", "Cc_3mm", "Cc_3hm"),
    labels = c(
      "dextromethorphan",
      "dextrorphan",
      "3-methoxymorphinan",
      "3-hydroxymorphinan"
    )
  ))

ggplot(sim_dex_long, aes(time, conc_nM, colour = species)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_y_continuous() +
  labs(
    x = "Time after dose (h)",
    y = "Plasma concentration (nmol/L)",
    colour = NULL,
    title = "Dextromethorphan + metabolites, typical-value, 30 mg PO single dose",
    caption = "Replicates the qualitative shape of Figure 3 of TerHeine 2014."
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

The shape replicates Figure 3 of the source paper: dextromethorphan
peaks early, dextrorphan rises quickly to dominate the systemic exposure
(consistent with the very large CL_CYP2D6,1 = 1560 L/h compared with
CL_CYP3A,1 = 44.7 L/h), 3-methoxymorphinan stays low (low formation
clearance from the parent), and 3-hydroxymorphinan builds slowly as the
terminal sink of both routes.

# Tamoxifen model -- typical-value simulation across CYP-phenotype scenarios

We exercise the tamoxifen model with a small set of CYP-phenotype
scenarios, all on the standard 20 mg PO QD chronic regimen at steady
state. The CYP2D6 / CYP3A4 covariate values are the individual probe-
derived metabolic activity scores (units: ng/L per the DDMORE bundle;
numerically equivalent to the individual post-hoc clearance values
derived from the dextromethorphan model above).

* **typical** -- population-median CYP2D6 (1560 ng/L) and CYP3A4 (44.7 ng/L);
* **CYP2D6-low** -- 156 ng/L (1/10 of the median, approximating poor-metabolizer activity);
* **CYP2D6-high** -- 15600 ng/L (10x the median, approximating ultrarapid-metabolizer activity);
* **CYP3A4-low** -- 4.47 ng/L (1/10 of the median);
* **CYP3A4-high** -- 447 ng/L (10x the median).

```{r tam-cohort}
scenarios <- tribble(
  ~scenario,        ~CYP2D6, ~CYP3A4,
  "typical",        1560,    44.7,
  "CYP2D6-low",      156,    44.7,
  "CYP2D6-high",   15600,    44.7,
  "CYP3A4-low",     1560,     4.47,
  "CYP3A4-high",    1560,    447
)

build_events <- function(scenarios) {
  obs_t <- seq(0, 24, by = 0.25)
  out <- vector("list", nrow(scenarios))
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(scenarios))) {
    sc   <- scenarios[i, ]
    n_obs <- length(obs_t)
    dose_row <- data.frame(
      id = i, time = 0, evid = 1, amt = 20, cmt = 1L, ii = 24, ss = 1,
      scenario = sc$scenario, CYP2D6 = sc$CYP2D6, CYP3A4 = sc$CYP3A4
    )
    obs_tam <- data.frame(
      id = i, time = obs_t, evid = 0, amt = 0, cmt = 4L, ii = 0, ss = 0,
      scenario = sc$scenario, CYP2D6 = sc$CYP2D6, CYP3A4 = sc$CYP3A4
    )
    obs_endx <- data.frame(
      id = i, time = obs_t, evid = 0, amt = 0, cmt = 5L, ii = 0, ss = 0,
      scenario = sc$scenario, CYP2D6 = sc$CYP2D6, CYP3A4 = sc$CYP3A4
    )
    out[[i]] <- rbind(dose_row, obs_tam, obs_endx)
  }
  dplyr::bind_rows(out)
}

events <- build_events(scenarios)
stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid", "cmt")])))
```

```{r tam-simulate}
mod_tam_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod_tam)

sim_tam <- rxode2::rxSolve(
  mod_tam_typ,
  events = events,
  keep   = c("scenario", "CYP2D6", "CYP3A4")
)
sim_tam <- as.data.frame(sim_tam)
```

```{r tam-profile, fig.width=7, fig.height=4}
sim_tam_parent <- sim_tam |>
  dplyr::filter(CMT == 4) |>
  dplyr::select(id, time, scenario, Cc)

ggplot(sim_tam_parent, aes(time, Cc, colour = scenario)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Time after dose (h)", y = "Tamoxifen Cc (nmol/L)",
    colour = "scenario",
    title = "Tamoxifen steady-state profile, 20 mg PO QD",
    caption = "Typical-value simulation; CYP covariates affect endoxifen formation only, so the parent profile is invariant across scenarios."
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r tam-endx-profile, fig.width=7, fig.height=4}
sim_endx <- sim_tam |>
  dplyr::filter(CMT == 5) |>
  dplyr::select(id, time, scenario, Cc_endx)

ggplot(sim_endx, aes(time, Cc_endx, colour = scenario)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Time after dose (h)", y = "Endoxifen Cc (nmol/L)",
    colour = "scenario",
    title = "Endoxifen steady-state profile, 20 mg PO QD",
    caption = "CYP2D6-high / CYP3A4-high increase endoxifen formation; the *-low scenarios reduce it."
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```

# PKNCA validation

## Dextromethorphan model -- single-dose NCA, parent + metabolites

```{r dex-nca}
sim_dex_nca <- sim_dex |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Cc, Cc_dxor, Cc_3mm, Cc_3hm),
    names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc"
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(conc)) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    id    = id,
    time  = time,
    conc  = conc,
    analyte = analyte
  )

# Ensure a time-zero record exists per analyte (PKNCA needs t = 0).
t0_rows <- sim_dex_nca |>
  dplyr::group_by(analyte) |>
  dplyr::summarise(time = min(time), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(needs_t0 = time > 0)

if (any(t0_rows$needs_t0)) {
  add_t0 <- t0_rows |>
    dplyr::filter(needs_t0) |>
    dplyr::transmute(id = 1, time = 0, conc = 0, analyte = analyte)
  sim_dex_nca <- dplyr::bind_rows(sim_dex_nca, add_t0) |>
    dplyr::arrange(analyte, time)
}

dose_df_dex <- data.frame(
  id      = 1,
  time    = 0,
  amt     = 30,
  analyte = unique(sim_dex_nca$analyte)
)

conc_obj_dex <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(
  sim_dex_nca, conc ~ time | analyte + id,
  concu = "nmol/L", timeu = "h"
)
dose_obj_dex <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(
  dose_df_dex, amt ~ time | analyte + id, doseu = "mg"
)

intervals_dex <- data.frame(
  start    = 0,
  end      = 22,
  cmax     = TRUE,
  tmax     = TRUE,
  auclast  = TRUE,
  half.life = TRUE
)

nca_dex <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(
  conc_obj_dex, dose_obj_dex, intervals = intervals_dex
))

dex_nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_dex$result) |>
  dplyr::select(analyte, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES)

knitr::kable(
  dex_nca_tbl,
  caption = "Single-dose NCA of typical-value dextromethorphan + metabolites (30 mg PO)."
)
```

The dextromethorphan NCA confirms the expected qualitative ordering:
dextrorphan exposure (Cmax, AUC) substantially exceeds the parent's
because CL_CYP2D6,1 (1560 L/h) is the dominant first-pass pathway, and
3-hydroxymorphinan accumulates as the terminal sink of both branches.

The source paper does NOT publish a clean NCA reference table for
dextromethorphan or its metabolites, so a direct numerical comparison
against published Cmax / AUC values is not possible. The visual
predictive checks in Figure 3 of the paper are the closest available
reference; the typical-value shapes in the simulation above match those
qualitative profiles.

## Tamoxifen model -- steady-state NCA over the dosing interval

```{r tam-nca-parent}
tau <- 24

sim_nca_tam <- sim_tam |>
  dplyr::filter(CMT == 4, !is.na(Cc)) |>
  dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, scenario)

dose_df_tam <- events |>
  dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>
  dplyr::select(id, time, amt, scenario)

conc_obj_tam <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(
  sim_nca_tam, Cc ~ time | scenario + id,
  concu = "nmol/L", timeu = "h"
)
dose_obj_tam <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(
  dose_df_tam, amt ~ time | scenario + id, doseu = "mg"
)

intervals_ss <- data.frame(
  start    = 0,
  end      = tau,
  cmax     = TRUE,
  tmax     = TRUE,
  cmin     = TRUE,
  auclast  = TRUE,
  cav      = TRUE,
  ctrough  = TRUE
)

nca_tam <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(
  conc_obj_tam, dose_obj_tam, intervals = intervals_ss
))

knitr::kable(
  as.data.frame(nca_tam$result) |>
    dplyr::select(scenario, id, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES),
  caption = "Steady-state NCA - tamoxifen (Cc) by CYP-phenotype scenario."
)
```

```{r tam-nca-endx}
sim_nca_endx <- sim_tam |>
  dplyr::filter(CMT == 5, !is.na(Cc_endx)) |>
  dplyr::select(id, time, Cc_endx, scenario) |>
  dplyr::rename(Cc = Cc_endx)

conc_obj_endx <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(
  sim_nca_endx, Cc ~ time | scenario + id,
  concu = "nmol/L", timeu = "h"
)

nca_endx <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(
  conc_obj_endx, dose_obj_tam, intervals = intervals_ss
))

knitr::kable(
  as.data.frame(nca_endx$result) |>
    dplyr::select(scenario, id, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES),
  caption = "Steady-state NCA - endoxifen (Cc_endx) by CYP-phenotype scenario."
)
```

The covariate sensitivities are the headline finding of ter Heine 2014:
endoxifen exposure rises with CYP2D6 activity (the CYP2D6-high scenario
yields a markedly higher endoxifen Cmax / AUC than CYP2D6-low) and rises
more modestly with CYP3A4 / 5 activity, while tamoxifen exposure is
invariant under either covariate (the CYP2D6 / CYP3A4 effects enter only
on the formation clearance to endoxifen).

The source paper does not publish a clean reference Cmax / Cmin / AUC
table for steady-state tamoxifen + endoxifen on 20 mg QD; the
deterministic typical-value profiles above are shown so a reviewer can
confirm the magnitudes are biologically plausible (tamoxifen
~100-300 nmol/L; endoxifen ~5-50 nmol/L for chronic 20 mg QD adults --
typical clinical-pharmacology ranges) and that the covariate
sensitivities go in the correct direction.

# Comparison against the DDMORE-shipped simulated dataset (tamoxifen)

The DDMORE bundle for `DDMODEL00000212` ships a simulated dataset
(`Simulated_tamoxifen.csv`, 100 hypothetical subjects, 20 mg QD steady
state) and a NONMEM re-fit (`Output_simulated_tamoxifen.lst`). The
re-fit's individual predictions cannot be matched row-by-row against a
typical-value simulation because the bundle carries Empirical-Bayes
etas. The cleanest sanity check the bundle does support is order-of-
magnitude: the simulated DV values for tamoxifen at steady state span
roughly 50-400 nmol/L and for endoxifen 3-15 nmol/L, which agrees with
the typical-value steady-state simulations above and with the
clinical-pharmacology literature for chronic 20 mg QD tamoxifen in adult
breast-cancer patients.

The DDMORE convertor implements the absorption lag inside the ODE
system rather than via the standard NONMEM `ALAG` mechanism; this
yields a materially different `tlag` estimate (2.02 h re-fit vs 0.455 h
published). The model file in this package uses the **publication**
values (matching paper Table 3); the convertor's `Output_simulated_tamoxifen.lst`
re-fit values are NOT used.

# Assumptions and deviations

- **Two model files, one vignette.** Per the `extract-literature-model`
  skill policy on multi-model papers, ter Heine 2014 contributes two
  independent `.R` files (one per published model) and one vignette
  covering the paper as a whole. Both `.R` files set
  `vignette <- "TerHeine_2014_tamoxifen"` to point here.

- **Dextromethorphan model: molar-units accounting.** The
  dextromethorphan model tracks all internal compartment amounts in
  nmol (with the molar-mass conversion applied once at the depot via
  `ratein <- ka * depot * 1e6 / mdex`), so the parent-to-metabolite
  1:1 molar conservation is exact across the four species (whose
  molecular masses range from 243 to 271 g/mol, a ~10% spread).
  Concentrations therefore come out directly in nmol/L = nM, matching
  the paper's LOQ of 0.5 nM and the reporting convention used
  throughout. The tamoxifen model uses a different convention (tracks
  in mg, converts to nmol/L at the output via the per-species MW);
  because the tamoxifen-vs-endoxifen MWs differ by only 0.5%, that
  approximation introduces negligible error.

- **Endoxifen disposition fixed from a separate publication.** The
  endoxifen elimination clearance (5.1 L/h) and central volume (400 L)
  in the tamoxifen model are fixed from Ahmad et al. (2010) Clin
  Pharmacol Ther 88(6):814-817, not estimated by ter Heine 2014. The
  fixed values are hard-coded inside `model()` rather than declared
  as `ini()` parameters with `fixed()` because they are literature
  constants, not parameters of the ter Heine 2014 fit. This convention
  is inherited from the existing tamoxifen model file (extracted from
  the DDMORE bundle).

- **No `CL_DO` (dextrorphan -> "other species") in the dextromethorphan
  model.** The Methods section mentions glucuronidation of dextrorphan
  ("this was accounted for by estimation of additional clearance of
  dextrorphan"), and Figure 2's caption references a parameter `CL_DO`
  for the dextrorphan-to-other-species pathway. However, the final-
  estimates Table 2 reports NO `CL_DO` value, and Appendix 1's ODE for
  the dextrorphan central compartment reads `dA3/dt = CL_CYP2D6,1 * CL
  - CL_CYP3A4,2 * C3` (only the CYP3A4-mediated onward step to
  3-hydroxymorphinan; no `CL_DO` term). The model file follows the
  Appendix 1 ODE faithfully.

- **Standard NONMEM `lag(depot)` for both models.** The DDMORE-converted
  `.mdl` for the tamoxifen model implemented the lag inside `$DES` via
  an `IF (T >= ALAG1)` switch; we use the standard `lag(depot)`
  mechanism (and the dextromethorphan model does the same). Both forms
  yield the same systemic exposure once t >= lag; the published
  parameters were originally fitted with the standard mechanism.

- **Residual-error correlation dropped (tamoxifen model).** The paper
  Table 3 reports a residual-error correlation between the parent and
  metabolite observations (rho = 62.2%). The DDMORE-implemented tamoxifen
  model file drops that correlation and parameterises the two SDs
  independently; re-introducing the correlated residual would require
  a multivariate residual-error formulation outside the standard
  nlmixr2 multi-output `prop()` syntax. This deviation is inherited
  from the existing tamoxifen model file.

- **Shared CYP2D6 IIV across `CL_2D6,1` and `CL_2D6,2`.** The paper
  states the inter-individual variability in the two CYP2D6 clearance
  parameters was highly correlated (>98%) and could not be estimated
  independently; a single shared IIV term was estimated for both.
  The dextromethorphan model file encodes this by applying the same
  `etalcl_2d6` to both `lcl_2d6_1` and `lcl_2d6_2`.

- **No estimated covariates in the dextromethorphan model.** The model
  is a typical-value semi-physiological structure used to derive
  per-subject CYP2D6 / CYP3A activity scores from individual
  post-hoc clearance estimates. Its `covariateData` block is therefore
  empty; the downstream tamoxifen model uses those derived activity
  scores as its own covariates (`CYP2D6`, `CYP3A4`).

- **CYP2D6 and CYP3A4 reference values (tamoxifen model).** The
  centering constants for the CYP2D6 (1560 ng/L) and CYP3A4 (44.7 ng/L)
  covariates in the tamoxifen model are the population-median individual
  post-hoc clearance values from the dextromethorphan model -- i.e.,
  numerically equal to the `CL_CYP2D6,1` (1560 L/h) and `CL_CYP3A,1`
  (44.7 L/h) point estimates in Table 2 of the source. The DDMORE
  bundle uses the units "ng/L" rather than "L/h" but the numbers
  agree.

- **Small (0.1%-level) numerical discrepancies on tamoxifen IIV / RUV
  values.** The existing DDMORE-derived tamoxifen `.R` file rounds IIV
  Vd_tam to 26.6% (paper Table 3: 26.7%), rho to 0.613 (Table 3: 0.612),
  IIV CL_MET to 25.5% (Table 3: 25.4%), and sigma_TAM to 0.138 (Table 3:
  13.7% i.e. 0.137). These differences are within the paper's three
  significant figures and are preserved to keep the model file aligned
  with the existing DDMORE-extraction provenance; they are listed in
  the source-trace table above for transparency.

- **`checkModelConventions()` warnings expected on the dextromethorphan
  model.** The CYP-named clearance parameters `lcl_2d6_1` /
  `lcl_2d6_2` / `lcl_3a4_1` / `lcl_3a4_2` / `lcl_hm` and the
  paper-numbered rate constants `lk57` / `lk75` / `lk68` / `lk86`
  are paper-faithful but not (yet) in the canonical parameter-names
  register. The four-output residual structure (`propSd` +
  `propSd_dxor` + `propSd_3mm` + `propSd_3hm`) is fine. The model
  file does not declare `dxor`, `3mm`, `3hm` as paper-specific
  compartments because they are registered as canonical
  metabolite-suffix tokens in `inst/references/compartment-names.md`
  (added alongside this extraction).
